notes: All substantive claims are supported by the cited corpus files. The Article 5 CDD obligation list (identification, verification, beneficial owner, purpose/nature, ongoing monitoring) matches mlo-article-5.md. The Article 6 trigger-event claim (CDD required at establishment, must complete before proceeding beyond preliminary steps) matches mlo-article-6.md. The Article 9 purpose-and-intended-nature detail and Article 10 ongoing-monitoring claims match mlo-part-3-cdd.md. The calibration for regulated-institution customers (bank/MiFID/AIFMD/Solvency-II/TCB, equivalent-jurisdiction test, SDD categories for listed/wholly-owned-sub/government-owned, reliance regime with four conditions including written arrangement and record-access on demand, accountability remaining with Jersey firm) all match amlh-customer-regulated-institution.md closely. The non-equivalent-jurisdiction reversion to full CDD with likely EDD is also supported by that file. All five cited files exist and are correctly characterised as draft status. The response appropriately flags draft status and directs the user to verify against live primary sources. One soft note: the response's phrasing 'Article 5 requires the supervised business to apply CDD measures comprising' slightly reorganises Article 5's structure (Article 5 lists the measures, Article 9 supplies the purpose/nature detail, Article 10 supplies the ongoing monitoring detail) but the draft's own text groups them together under Article 5, so this is faithful to the corpus even if the corpus itself simplifies. No hallucinated citations, no unsupported claims, no authority-tier violations detected.
